preamble: The shift from a low nasalized vowel [ã] to a high back rounded nasalized vowel [ũ] involves both raising and rounding, often as a result of vowel chain shifts or assimilation to nearby labial/velar segments.

examples.note: Raising and rounding under the influence of nasalization in specific linguistic environments.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
